text.title: Capacity Planning at Design Time Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Capacity Planning at Design Time` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Spec MUST list peak RPS forecast with the source funnel + assumptions. `we'll figure it out` is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit RPS forecast, autoscaling ceilings and reservation purchases are guesswork.

rule.statement: Production target utilisation MUST be ≤ 60% at forecast peak (40% headroom). Bursty workloads MUST be ≤ 40% (60% headroom).
rule.rationale: Running > 60% utilisation eats the headroom you need for spike absorption + rolling restarts.

rule.statement: Spec MUST publish the autoscaling ceiling (max replicas / max nodes) and the business-justification for that ceiling. `unlimited` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unlimited autoscale means a bug becomes a bill; ceiling caps both blast radius and budget.

rule.statement: If reserved instances / capacity-blocks are required, spec MUST list region, instance family, count, term, and named owner of the renewal.

rationale.source: AWS Savings Plans + Azure reservation guides

rule.rationale: Reserved capacity is a multi-year financial commitment; anonymous reservations get orphaned.

rule.statement: Spec MUST list a named SRE owner accountable for the capacity numbers, reviewable each quarter.
rule.rationale: Anonymous capacity numbers rot; named owner forces refresh cadence.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
